projects.name: Bosch Future Mobility Challenge
projects.description: Led a 5-person team in a global autonomous driving competition through an iterative development cycle, ranking as a top 20 finalist out of 80+ teams. Designed a perception-decision pipeline using hierarchical state machines. Manually curated and annotated data for MobileNet V2 models, optimized for real-time inference on a Raspberry Pi under strict resource constraints.

projects.name: Unsupervised Object-Centric Learning
projects.description: Architected an unsupervised vision system using Slot Attention to automate nodule segmentation. Engineered a scalable, high-throughput pipeline to curate and normalize 7,980 multi-modal CT cases from heterogeneous sources, ensuring high-fidelity training data.

projects.name: Distributed Systems Engineering
projects.description: Architected a local-first distributed application using Node.js and ZeroMQ to achieve high availability and horizontal scalability. Implemented a consistent hashing ring with virtual nodes for optimal load balancing and utilized Conflict-Free Replicated Data Types (CRDTs) to ensure eventual consistency across distributed replicas.

projects.name: Low-Level Systems & Hardware Design
projects.description: Developed a real-time interactive application in C prioritizing hardware efficiency. Directly handled concurrent signals from multiple I/O devices (mouse, keyboard, serial port) via hardware interrupts. Managed asynchronous data streams using concurrent software FIFOs to ensure deterministic synchronization, demonstrating strong foundations in low-level parallelization.

projects.name: AI-Driven Search Engine (ChessKoban)
projects.description: Developed an AI-powered puzzle game in Python that merges Chess mechanics with Sokoban-inspired logic. Implemented multiple uninformed and heuristic search algorithms (BFS, DFS, Greedy, and A*) to solve complex board states. Engineered an autonomous 'Computer Mode' and a real-time 'Tip System', leveraging NetworkX for efficient graph-based pathfinding.

projects.name: Algorithmic Optimization (TSP)
projects.description: Addressed the Travelling Salesperson Problem (TSP) by implementing and comparing multiple algorithms. Developed a brute-force method for small graphs, a triangular approximation heuristic, and a highly efficient custom heuristic for large, fully connected graphs, thoroughly analyzing computational trade-offs between solution quality and execution time.

projects.name: Enterprise HR Platform (Kuehne + Nagel)
projects.description: Conducted stakeholder meetings to translate ambiguous requirements into biweekly sprint plans and technical workflows. Architected a robust database schema and RESTful APIs, prioritizing long-term scalability. Delivered a functional platform meeting all non-technical HR requirements for a global enterprise.
